metadata.title: Nora Soini – Therapist & Craniosacral Therapy Espoo
metadata.description: Short-term therapist in presence and systemic approaches and craniosacral therapy student, based in Espoo.

hero.heading: Gently by your side through change
hero.description: Clarity, peace and connection to yourself. Together we create space for you to pause, breathe and truly hear yourself. With gentle support – at your own pace.

situations.heading: Situations I can help with
situations.description: Every situation is unique. You are welcome to come, for example, when:

cards.title: Stress, strain or exhaustion
cards.description: When life feels heavy or you tire easily, or when your body and mind need room to settle and recover.

cards.title: A turning point in life
cards.description: You are in transition – a new chapter, a decision or a change is asking for space and to be truly heard.

cards.title: Clarity and direction
cards.description: You long for clarity or a sense of direction: you want to recognise your own wishes and make choices that feel right.

cards.title: Deeper self-understanding
cards.description: You want to understand yourself and your experiences more deeply – in a gentle, safe pace, without rush.

cards.title: Gentle somatic support
cards.description: You hope for gentle, body-based support for recovery – space to listen to your body, your feelings and your nervous system.

servicesSlider.heading: Services
servicesSlider.description: Short-term therapy, craniosacral therapy, and combination sessions.

about.quote: "I believe every person already holds within them a direction towards balance."
about.heading: My work is calm, client-centered, and confidential.
about.description: I am currently training as a craniosacral therapist, graduating autumn 2026. During my studies I offer practice sessions at an affordable price. I am also training as an energy healer, graduating spring 2027.
about.description2: What matters most in my work is a safe, calm space where a person can be met as a whole – body, emotions and mind.
about.description3: My path to this work has run through a long journey of personal growth. My own experiences have taught me a great deal about humanity and how a person can find their way back to connection with themselves.

pillars.heading: Three cornerstones of my practice
pillars.description: I combine conversation, presence and somatic listening to create a holistic and safe space.

items.title: Conversation
items.description: Together we explore feelings, bodily experiences and the relationships and life history that shape you – in a genuine, safe encounter. You get to talk about whatever feels most important right now.

items.title: Presence
items.description: A moment where you get to be seen and heard exactly as you are – without hurry or demands. Presence itself is a healing experience.

items.title: Body awareness
items.description: Craniosacral therapy calms the nervous system through gentle touch. The body holds wisdom that words cannot always reach.

process.heading: How to get started
process.description: The first step of reaching out can feel big – that is why I want to make it as easy as possible.

steps.title: Free introductory call
steps.description: A short, non-committal 15-minute call. You can share your situation and ask me anything.

steps.title: Book a first appointment
steps.description: You can also book directly. At the first session we will calmly go through your situation and wishes.

steps.title: Send a message
steps.description: Not sure which option suits you? Send me a message and I will help you find the right way to start.

faq.heading: Answers to common questions
faq.description: It is completely normal for therapy or a new treatment to raise questions. You can also reach me at nora@norasoini.fi

items.question: How do I know if I need therapy?
items.answer: If something is weighing on your mind or your life situation feels burdensome, therapy can help. You don't need a 'big enough' problem. The first meeting is a free introduction.

items.question: Is therapy confidential?
items.answer: Yes. Everything discussed in therapy sessions is confidential. I am bound by professional secrecy.

items.question: How long does therapy last?
items.answer: Short-term therapy is typically 5–20 sessions. The length of the process is planned individually according to your needs.

items.question: How much does therapy cost?
items.answer: Short-term therapy sessions are €89 / 60 min. Short-term therapy is not currently covered by Kela reimbursement, but it is worth checking with your employer or insurance company.

items.question: Can sessions be arranged remotely?
items.answer: Yes, short-term therapy sessions can be arranged remotely via video call. Craniosacral therapy is always in Espoo.

items.question: Do I need to know anything in particular?
items.answer: Nothing in particular. Come as you are.

items.question: What is craniosacral therapy?
items.answer: Craniosacral therapy is a gentle, calm form of treatment in which the body is listened to through light touch. The aim is to release tension, support nervous system balance and promote the body's own recovery. Craniosacral therapy does not replace a doctor's examination, treatment or other healthcare services, and no medical diagnosis is made.

items.question: What is the treatment like in practice?
items.answer: The treatment is carried out fully clothed, lying on a therapy table. Touch is very light and the session progresses calmly, listening to the client's body. Many people find the treatment deeply relaxing.

items.question: Who is this treatment for?
items.answer: People often come to craniosacral therapy when there is strain, stress, restlessness or chronic tension in body or mind. The treatment can help you pause, calm down and strengthen your connection to your own body.

items.question: The idea behind the treatment
items.answer: Craniosacral therapy holds that the body has a natural capacity to move towards balance. The treatment supports this natural recovery gently and individually.

items.question: What is LST therapy based on?
items.answer: Presence and Systemic Therapy (LST) combines several therapeutic approaches: family constellation, emotion-focused therapy (EFT), ACT (acceptance and commitment therapy), solution-focused therapy and mindfulness. The person is seen as a whole.

items.question: Is craniosacral therapy covered by insurance?
items.answer: Craniosacral therapy does not replace a doctor's examination or other healthcare services, and no medical diagnosis is made. The treatment is not covered by Kela reimbursement.

metadata.title: Short-term therapy and craniosacral therapy in Espoo and online – Nora Soini
metadata.description: I offer short-term therapy in Espoo and remotely, craniosacral therapy in Espoo, and holistic combination sessions that bring these together. You can choose what suits you best – conversation, body-based treatment, or both.

hero.heading: Short-term therapy and craniosacral therapy in Espoo and online
hero.ingress: I offer short-term therapy in Espoo and remotely, craniosacral therapy in Espoo, and holistic combination sessions that bring these together. You can choose what suits you best – conversation, body-based treatment, or both.

services.cardQuote: You are welcome to reach out – I will help you find the right option for you.

visitFlow.heading: How does a session unfold?
visitFlow.description: We begin with a gentle conversation, then move into craniosacral therapy. This allows both clarity of mind and deep relaxation of the body.

steps.title: Arrival and settling in
steps.description: When you arrive, we take a moment to settle. You don't need to have anything figured out – you can come exactly as you are.

steps.title: Gentle conversation
steps.description: We begin with a calm conversation. You can share what is on your mind or what brought you here today.

steps.title: Craniosacral therapy
steps.description: We move into body-based care. Gentle touch calms the nervous system and allows both clarity of mind and deep relaxation of the body.

items.title: Short-term therapy in Espoo and remotely
items.intro: Goal-oriented, practical conversational support that helps you organise your thoughts, process strain, and find a way forward.
items.body:
Short-term therapy is goal-oriented, practical conversational support that helps you organise your thoughts, process strain, and find a way forward.

- Sessions in Espoo or online
- Duration: 60 min
- Price: €89 per session

**Short-term therapy may suit you if:**

- you feel stress, exhaustion or anxiety
- you long for clarity in your life situation
- you want a safe space to speak and be heard

items.title: Craniosacral therapy in Espoo
items.intro: A gentle, deeply relaxing treatment that calms the nervous system and helps the body recover.
items.body:
Craniosacral therapy is a gentle, deeply relaxing treatment that calms the nervous system and helps the body recover.

- Sessions in Espoo
- Duration: 60 min
- Student rate: €69 per session (valid until end of August 2026)
- Standard rate: €95 per session (from 1 September 2026)

**This treatment may suit you if:**

- your body feels tense or wired
- you suffer from stress or find recovery difficult
- you long for deep relaxation

items.title: Combination session: short-term therapy + craniosacral therapy in Espoo
items.intro: Holistic care for both mind and body in one visit – conversational support together with body-based treatment.
items.body:
In combination work, both mind and body are considered. In one visit you receive conversational support and body-based treatment.

This is especially helpful when strain is present both in your thoughts and in your body.

- Sessions in Espoo
- Duration: 90 min
- Price: €129 per session

**Combination sessions may suit you if:**

- you feel strain or exhaustion on every level
- your body and mind need support at the same time
- talking alone or bodywork alone does not feel enough

metadata.title: I am Nora Soini, a short-term therapist in presence and systemic approaches from Espoo.
metadata.description: Training as a craniosacral therapist (graduating autumn 2026) and as an energy healer (spring 2027). Short-term therapist based in Espoo.

hero.heading: I am Nora Soini, a short-term therapist in presence and systemic approaches from Espoo.
hero.ingress: I am currently training to become a craniosacral therapist, graduating in autumn 2026. During my studies I offer practice sessions at an affordable rate. I am also studying to become an energy healer, graduating in spring 2027.

intro.ingressBelowPhoto: I am in my early sixties, a mother of two adult children, and I have been in a long marriage. Life has taught me a great deal about humanity, relationships and how deeply our experience shapes us.
paragraphsBeforeBento: In 2011 I became an entrepreneur. Before entrepreneurship I worked for many years in an international company.

My path to this work has been one of long personal growth. Through my own therapy I discovered the helper within myself years ago. When your own inner work goes deep enough, a desire often arises to also be there for others on their journey.

afterBento.ingress: Along my life I have also faced deep challenges.
paragraphs: As a teenager my parents divorced; as a young adult I was rejected without explanation; and as an adult I was subjected to prolonged bullying. These experiences left their mark, but they have also taught me a great deal about being human, vulnerability and how a person can find their way back to connection with themselves.

What matters most in my work is a safe, calm space where a person can be met as a whole; body, emotions and mind. I believe each person already holds within them a direction towards balance and wholeness. Sometimes we only need a moment to pause and be seen on our journey.

slides.quote: Is there a moment in your life when something no longer feels like it used to?
slides.description: Perhaps you are tired, overwhelmed or unsure of your direction. Perhaps you carry experiences that still affect your everyday life. You are not alone.

slides.quote: I offer you a calm and safe space to pause.
slides.description: A space where you can be met exactly as you are – without hurry, without demands.

slides.quote: Our work brings together conversation, presence and somatic listening.
slides.description: Together we can explore what is happening within you, and make room for change at your own pace.

slides.quote: Often, simply pausing for a moment can begin to change something.
slides.description: In treatment and therapy the goal is not to fix you, but to help you find your way back to connection with yourself, your feelings and your body's wisdom.

contactForm.successMessage: Thank you for your message! I will be in touch soon.
contactForm.errorMessage: Something went wrong. Please try again.

faq.title: Answers to common questions
faq.description: It is completely normal for therapy to raise questions.

items.question: How do I know if I need therapy?
items.answer: If something is weighing on your mind, your life situation feels overwhelming, or you find yourself going over the same thoughts again and again – therapy can be exactly the right place to pause. You don't need a crisis or a diagnosis. It's enough that something in your life is calling for space to be heard.

items.question: Is therapy confidential?
items.answer: Yes, absolutely. Everything discussed in therapy sessions is completely confidential. I am bound by professional confidentiality, and your information will never be shared without your consent. A sense of safety and trust is the very foundation of our work together.

items.question: Do I need to know anything in particular?
items.answer: Not at all. You don't need any prior experience with therapy or a specific reason to come. You arrive as you are, in this very moment – and that is entirely enough. The first session is always a calm introduction where we explore together what you need.

items.question: What is craniosacral therapy?
items.answer: Craniosacral therapy is a gentle, quiet form of treatment in which the body is listened to through very light touch. The aim is to release tension, support the balance of the nervous system and promote the body's own recovery. It does not replace medical examination or treatment, and no diagnosis is made.

items.question: What is the treatment like in practice?
items.answer: The treatment is carried out fully clothed, lying on a therapy table – there is nothing you need to do. Touch is very light, almost imperceptible, and the session progresses calmly, following the body's own rhythms. Many people find the treatment deeply relaxing; some fall asleep during the session.

items.question: Who is this treatment for?
items.answer: People often come to craniosacral therapy when there is strain, stress, restlessness or chronic tension in body or mind. It is also a good fit for anyone who simply needs space to slow down in the middle of a busy life. The treatment can help strengthen your connection to your own body and foster a sense of overall wellbeing.

items.question: The idea behind the treatment
items.answer: Craniosacral therapy is built on the idea that the body has a natural capacity to move towards balance, when given support and stillness. The treatment does not force or fix – it listens and creates space. This makes it a particularly gentle way of approaching the wellbeing of both body and mind.

items.question: How long does therapy last?
items.answer: Short-term therapy is typically 5–20 sessions, usually meeting once a week or every two weeks. The duration always depends on your individual needs – for some, a few sessions are enough, while others benefit from a longer process. We will agree on a suitable rhythm together and review how things are going along the way.

items.question: Can sessions be arranged remotely?
items.answer: Yes, therapy sessions can be arranged remotely via video call. Remote sessions are a good option if you are unable to come in person or prefer to work from home. Craniosacral treatments are always in-person.

items.question: Do I need to prepare for the meeting?
items.answer: No special preparation is needed. If you can, it is nice to take a moment to settle before the session – but it is not required. Come as you are, in whatever state you happen to be in. That is completely enough.

items.question: How much does therapy cost?
items.answer: Sessions are €89. Short-term therapy is not currently covered by Kela reimbursement, so it is paid for out of pocket. Some employers cover therapy as part of occupational wellbeing benefits – it is worth checking your employer's policy.

items.question: How can I book an appointment?
items.answer: You can book by emailing nora@norasoini.fi or filling in the contact form on this website. I typically reply within 1–2 business days and we will find a time that works for you.

items.question: How can I pay?
items.answer: Sessions are paid by bank transfer according to the invoice. The invoice is sent by email after the session, with a 14-day payment term.

items.question: How do I cancel an appointment?
items.answer: Cancellations are made by email at nora@norasoini.fi. Please let me know as soon as possible so the slot can be offered to someone else who needs it.

items.question: What happens if I cancel late?
items.answer: Please cancel at least 24 hours before the appointment. Late cancellations will be charged the full session fee, as the time can no longer be filled. I understand that unexpected things happen – if you have a particular reason, we can discuss it case by case.
